evidence.excerpt: OUTPUT_ARCH(riscv); ENTRY(_entry); BASE_ADDRESS = 0x80020000;

evidence.excerpt: .section .text.entry; .globl _entry; _entry: ... la sp, boot_stack; add sp, sp, t0; call main; loop: j loop
evidence.summary: _entry sets a per-hart boot stack from a0 and calls main.

evidence.excerpt: main(unsigned long hartid, unsigned long dtb_pa) { inithartid(hartid); ... kvminit(); kvminithart(); ... trapinithart();
evidence.summary: The C handoff target is main(), which performs early per-hart and subsystem initialization.

evidence.excerpt: OUTPUT_ARCH(riscv); ENTRY(_start); BASE_ADDRESS = 0x80020000;
evidence.summary: An alternate k210 linker script exists with _start, but the Makefile line 40 selects linker64.ld.

Searched ENTRY, _start, boot, linker, BSS, UART, SBI, OpenSBI, U-Boot, hart, platform, Makefile, Cargo across kernel, linker, include, doc and Makefile; 214 files considered and 628 matches. The build-selected entry is linker64.ld/_entry plus kernel/entry.S.

answer.value: Active kernel boot entry is repos/xv6-k210/linker/linker64.ld ENTRY(_entry), implemented by repos/xv6-k210/kernel/entry.S symbol _entry. _entry computes a per-hart stack from a0, sets sp to boot_stack plus the per-hart offset, then calls main() in kernel/main.c. There is also an alternate k210.ld/entry_k210.S pair with _start, but the current Makefile selects linker64.ld and entry.S.
answer.notes: The answer distinguishes build-selected entry from present-but-not-selected alternate entry files.

answer.value: 固件/引导加载器 → 内核入口（如 SBI/OpenSBI/U-Boot/BIOS/UEFI）
answer.notes: The concrete firmware is SBI/RustSBI; U-Boot was not found in the active chain.

evidence.excerpt: SSTATUS_SPP, SSTATUS_SPIE, SSTATUS_SIE, SSTATUS_UIE
evidence.summary: sstatus privilege and interrupt-enable bits are defined.

evidence.excerpt: intr_off(); w_stvec(...uservec...); x &= ~SSTATUS_SPP; x |= SSTATUS_SPIE; w_sstatus(x); w_sepc(...); satp = MAKE_SATP(...)
evidence.summary: Return-to-user path programs stvec, sstatus, sepc and satp.

evidence.excerpt: SATP_SV39; MAKE_SATP(...); w_satp(...); r_satp()
evidence.summary: SATP and page-table CSR helpers are defined.

answer.value: Key registers/bits are sstatus.SPP (cleared to select User as previous privilege), sstatus.SPIE (set so interrupts are enabled after sret), stvec (switched between kernelvec and uservec), sepc (saved user PC), and satp (page-table root via MAKE_SATP).
answer.notes: These registers are directly written in the return-to-user path.

DTB parsing: not_found [main has dtb_pa parameter but no dtb/fdt parser use found]. Page Table Init: implemented before MMU enable [main calls kvminit then kvminithart].

evidence.summary: Default platform is k210.

evidence.excerpt: $(LD) ... -T $(linker); cargo build --no-default-features --features=$(platform)
evidence.summary: Kernel linker and SBI build feature are selected in Makefile.

answer.value: Makefile drives selection. platform defaults to k210. If platform=qemu, it adds -D QEMU and uses sbi-qemu; otherwise it uses sbi-k210. The kernel entry file and linker are fixed as kernel/entry.S and linker/linker64.ld in the current Makefile. Platform-specific HAL sources differ: k210 adds spi/gpiohs/fpioa/sdcard/dmac/sysctl/utils, while qemu adds virtio_disk.c. The SBI sub-build receives cargo --features=$(platform).
answer.notes: The build does not conditionally switch between entry.S and entry_k210.S.

evidence.excerpt: trapinithart(): w_stvec((uint64)kernelvec);
evidence.summary: Kernel traps initially enter kernelvec.

evidence.excerpt: kernelvec saves registers and calls kerneltrap.
evidence.summary: Kernel trap assembly entry saves context and calls C handler.

evidence.excerpt: uservec saves user registers in trapframe and jumps to usertrap.
evidence.summary: User trap assembly entry is uservec.

evidence.excerpt: usertrap handles syscall, interrupts and exceptions then calls usertrapret.
evidence.summary: C user trap handler.

answer.value: Kernel-mode trap vector: kernel/trap/kernelvec.S symbol kernelvec, installed by trapinithart() with w_stvec(kernelvec), then calls kerneltrap(). User-mode trap vector: kernel/trap/trampoline.S symbol uservec, installed by usertrapret() as TRAMPOLINE+(uservec-trampoline), then jumps to usertrap().
answer.notes: There is one kernel vector and one trampoline user vector, selected by stvec.

evidence.excerpt: per-process data for trap handling; sscratch points here; uservec saves registers in trapframe; usertrapret/userret restore user registers
evidence.summary: trapframe is per-process data used by trampoline.

evidence.excerpt: p->kstack = allocpage(); p->trapframe = kmalloc(sizeof(struct trapframe)); proc_pagetable(p)
evidence.summary: Process allocation creates a kernel stack and a per-process trapframe object.

evidence.excerpt: sscratch points to p->trapframe; csrrw a0, sscratch, a0; save user registers in TRAPFRAME
evidence.summary: Trampoline uses sscratch/a0 as trapframe pointer.

evidence.excerpt: fn = TRAMPOLINE + (userret - trampoline); fn((uint64)(p->trapframe), satp);
evidence.summary: usertrapret passes p->trapframe into userret.

answer.value: 用户地址空间预留页（trampoline/trap_context page）
answer.notes: Closest choice: the trampoline uses a per-process trapframe pointer via sscratch. The current code allocates p->trapframe with kmalloc, so it is not literally only a fixed TRAPFRAME page.

evidence.excerpt: struct trapframe fields from offset 0 through offset 544 fcsr.
evidence.summary: Trapframe structure definition with byte offsets.

evidence.excerpt: uservec saves ra, sp, gp, tp, t0..t6, s0..s11, a0..a7 into offsets 40..280
evidence.summary: Integer registers are saved into trapframe.

evidence.excerpt: ft0..ft11, fs0..fs11, fa0..fa7, fcsr
evidence.summary: Floating-point register slots are included.

answer.value: TrapFrame is struct trapframe in repos/xv6-k210/include/trap.h. It has 5 kernel/control uint64 fields, 31 integer user-register fields (ra/sp/gp/tp, t0-t6, s0-s11, a0-a7), 32 floating-point register fields, and fcsr. The final annotated offset is 544 for fcsr, so the structure occupies 552 bytes if packed as listed, normally rounded by C alignment to 552 bytes because all fields are uint64.
answer.notes: Register count excludes the five kernel metadata/control words.

answer.value: implemented

evidence.excerpt: entry(name): li a7, SYS_name; ecall; ret
evidence.summary: User syscall stubs place syscall number in a7 and execute ecall.

evidence.excerpt: entry("read"); entry("write"); entry("close");
evidence.summary: write user stub is generated.

evidence.excerpt: if cause == EXCP_ENV_CALL ... epc += 4; intr_on(); syscall();
evidence.summary: User ecall reaches usertrap and calls syscall().

evidence.excerpt: [SYS_write] sys_write
evidence.summary: Dispatch table maps SYS_write to sys_write.

evidence.excerpt: sys_write gets fd, pointer, length and returns filewrite(f, p, n)
evidence.summary: sys_write implementation.

evidence.excerpt: filewrite checks writable/range and writes to pipe, device or inode.
evidence.summary: sys_write continues into filewrite.

answer.value: write path: user stub generated by usys.pl sets a7=SYS_write and executes ecall -> usertrap sees EXCP_ENV_CALL, advances epc and calls syscall() -> syscall table dispatches [SYS_write] to sys_write -> sys_write extracts fd/buffer/len and calls filewrite -> filewrite validates and writes to pipe/device/inode.
answer.notes: This traces one concrete syscall end to end.

evidence.excerpt: syscall table registers fork, execve, openat, write, mmap, munmap, kill, rt_sigaction and others.
evidence.summary: High-value syscalls are registered.

evidence.excerpt: sys_exec/sys_execve call execve; sys_fork calls clone; sys_clone calls clone
evidence.summary: Process syscalls have bodies.

evidence.excerpt: sys_write and sys_openat implementations call filewrite/create/nameifrom/fdalloc.
evidence.summary: File syscalls have bodies.

evidence.excerpt: sys_mmap calls do_mmap; sys_munmap calls do_munmap.
evidence.summary: mmap/munmap have bodies.

evidence.excerpt: sys_kill gets pid/sig and calls kill(pid, sig).
evidence.summary: kill syscall has a body.

evidence.excerpt: sys_getuid returns 0; sys_prlimit64 returns 0 with comment may be implemented later.
evidence.summary: Some registered syscalls are stubs or shallow compatibility implementations.

answer.value: High-value syscall status: fork implemented [sys_fork->clone]; exec/execve implemented [calls execve]; mmap implemented [sys_mmap->do_mmap]; openat implemented [name lookup/create/fd allocation]; write implemented [sys_write->filewrite]; read implemented [sys_read->fileread]; kill implemented [sys_kill->kill]; wait/wait4 implemented [wait4 path]; rt_sigaction implemented but partial [copies handler only]; getuid/geteuid/getgid/getegid and prlimit64 are stub/shallow compatibility returns.
answer.notes: The listed syscalls are registered; the final group is intentionally shallow.

evidence.summary: copyout and copyout2 validate user address access.

evidence.summary: copyin path validates segments.

evidence.summary: Syscall argument fetching uses user copy helpers.

evidence.excerpt: fileread/filewrite check rangeinseg(addr, addr+n).
evidence.summary: File IO checks user buffer ranges.

answer.value: implemented
answer.notes: The mechanism is named copyin2/copyout2/rangeinseg rather than access_ok.

evidence.summary: Timer interrupt handler updates timer and process accounting.

evidence.excerpt: else if (0 == handle_intr(cause)) { if (yield()) p->ivswtch += 1; }
evidence.summary: After handling a user interrupt, usertrap calls yield.

evidence.excerpt: yield removes current proc, reinserts it runnable, and sched()s.
evidence.summary: yield performs preemptive scheduling if another proc is runnable.

answer.value: implemented
answer.notes: For user traps, any handled interrupt, including timer, reaches yield; kerneltrap itself does not yield.

evidence.excerpt: if (p->killed) { if SIGTERM exit; sighandle(); } usertrapret();
evidence.summary: Pending signal handling runs before returning to user mode.

evidence.excerpt: sighandle chooses pending signal, allocates sig_frame/trapframe, sets epc to SIG_TRAMPOLINE sig_handler and p->trapframe=tf.
evidence.summary: Signal delivery rewrites trapframe to enter signal trampoline.

evidence.excerpt: sigreturn restores previous p->trapframe from sig_frame and frees frame.
evidence.summary: Signal return restores saved trapframe.

evidence.excerpt: if SYS_rt_sigreturn == num: sigreturn();
evidence.summary: rt_sigreturn is special-cased in syscall dispatcher.

answer.value: implemented
answer.notes: Signal delivery is tied to trap return and rt_sigreturn restores the old trapframe.

evidence.excerpt: handle_excp maps load/store/inst page/access faults to handle_page_fault(kind, r_stval()).
evidence.summary: Trap exception handler delegates page faults to memory module.

evidence.excerpt: handle_store_page_fault_cow checks refcount, copies page if needed, sets PTE_W and clears PTE_COW.
evidence.summary: CoW page fault handling is implemented.

evidence.excerpt: handle_page_fault_lazy allocates a page for badaddr and sfence_vma.
evidence.summary: Lazy allocation fault handling is implemented.

evidence.excerpt: seg->type LOAD/HEAP/STACK/MMAP dispatches to loadelf, lazy, or handle_page_fault_mmap.
evidence.summary: Page fault handling dispatches by memory segment type.

answer.value: Yes. trap.c handle_excp decodes page/access faults and calls handle_page_fault(kind, r_stval()). vm.c then handles CoW store faults, lazy HEAP/STACK allocation, LOAD-on-demand, and MMAP faults through segment-type dispatch.
answer.notes: Illegal accesses that do not match a segment or legal fault path return failure to the trap layer.

evidence.excerpt: _entry derives stack offset from a0, uses boot_stack, .space 4096*4*2.
evidence.summary: Boot stack is per-hart sized for two harts.

evidence.excerpt: hart 0 initializes; sends sbi_send_ipi to other harts; other harts wait for started then run floatinithart/kvminithart/trapinithart.
evidence.summary: AP startup order is explicit in main.

evidence.excerpt: shrink the boot stack; freepage(kstack + PGSIZE*i); scheduler();
evidence.summary: After boot each hart frees most of its boot stack and enters scheduler.

evidence.excerpt: floatinithart(); kvminithart(); trapinithart();
evidence.summary: Secondary harts install MMU and trap vector before scheduling.

answer.value: Consistent for a two-hart design: entry.S computes a per-hart boot-stack offset, hart 0 initializes global subsystems and sends SBI IPIs, secondary harts wait for started and then run floatinithart, kvminithart and trapinithart before entering scheduler. There is no separate per-CPU trap stack; kernel traps use the current kernel stack.
answer.notes: This answers the boot/trap side of the SMP consistency question.

evidence.excerpt: static uint64 (*syscalls[])(void) has 69 initializer rows, with SYS_getpid appearing twice.
evidence.summary: Registered syscall table entries counted from source.

evidence.excerpt: sysnum.h defines 71 SYS_* names, including SYS_rename, SYS_syslog and SYS_rt_sigreturn.
evidence.summary: Syscall number definitions counted from source.

evidence.excerpt: SYS_rt_sigreturn is special-cased; table entries are dispatched; missing entries return -1.
evidence.summary: rt_sigreturn is not table-registered but is handled; missing entries return -1.

evidence.excerpt: sys_getuid returns 0; sys_prlimit64 returns 0 with comment may be implemented later.
evidence.summary: Representative stub/shallow syscalls.

evidence.excerpt: sys_adjtimex returns 0.
evidence.summary: Another stub example.

answer.value: 分发表路径: repos/xv6-k210/kernel/syscall/syscall.c. 完整实现: about 62-63 non-stub registered/special syscalls. 桩/浅兼容: about 6-7, examples getuid/geteuid/getgid/getegid alias sys_getuid returning 0, prlimit64 returning 0, adjtimex returning 0, and setitimer effectively not arming a timer. 未注册: 2 names from sysnum.h are not table-dispatched/special-cased: SYS_rename and SYS_syslog. 统计依据: sysnum.h has 71 SYS_* names; syscall.c has 69 initializer rows but one duplicate SYS_getpid, plus special SYS_rt_sigreturn handling.
answer.notes: Exact implemented-vs-shallow count is an estimate because some compatibility syscalls validate arguments but intentionally implement limited semantics.

evidence.excerpt: README.md exists in the repo.
evidence.summary: README exists, but the syscall detail is mostly in docs.

evidence.excerpt: Document describes syscall() and the syscalls[] table in kernel/syscall.c.
evidence.summary: Documentation claims the table-based syscall mechanism.

evidence.excerpt: Document says xv6-k210 added many syscalls; examples include rename/move and notes root-like direct returns for multi-user related syscalls.
evidence.summary: Syscall v2 docs discuss added compatibility syscalls and shallow multi-user returns.

evidence.excerpt: Actual syscalls[] table entries.
evidence.summary: Code is the source of truth for registered syscalls.

answer.value: README files exist, but detailed syscall claims are in doc/构建调试-系统调用*.md rather than a single README table. The docs correctly describe user stubs, ecall, usertrap, syscall(), and syscalls[] dispatch. The v2 doc says many compatibility syscalls were added and notes some multi-user calls directly return root-like values; code confirms a broad table but also confirms shallow entries such as getuid-style calls and prlimit64/adjtimex.
answer.notes: Because the README is not the main syscall claim source, I used the repo syscall docs plus code table for comparison.

answer.notes: Normal syscall names are sys_write, sys_mmap, etc., without _impl suffix.

evidence.summary: PLIC init is called during boot.

answer.value: implemented
answer.notes: This is distinct from timer interrupt handling; device IRQs are claimed/completed through PLIC.

evidence.excerpt: Defines SIGTERM, SIGKILL, SIGABRT, SIGHUP, SIGINT, SIGQUIT, SIGILL, SIGTRAP, SIGCHLD; no SIGSEGV.
evidence.summary: Signal header does not define SIGSEGV.

evidence.excerpt: unhandled user trap prints unexpected scause and sets p->killed = SIGTERM.
evidence.summary: Unexpected illegal access kills with SIGTERM, not SIGSEGV.
Searched SIGSEGV, sigsegv, sig_segv and SEGV across allowed source/docs; match_count=0.

answer.value: not_found
answer.notes: Illegal/unhandled user trap paths set SIGTERM; SIGSEGV is not defined or delivered.

evidence.excerpt: sys_kill reads pid and sig, then return kill(pid, sig).
evidence.summary: Process-level kill syscall exists.

evidence.excerpt: kill searches proc by pid, sets sig_pending and killed, wakes sleeping proc.
evidence.summary: kill targets a process id.
Searched sys_tkill, sys_tgkill, tkill, tgkill across kernel/include/user/docs; match_count=0.

answer.value: Supported signal-send granularity is process-level by pid: sys_kill(pid, sig) calls kill(pid, sig), which searches the process table by pid and marks sig_pending/killed. I found no tkill/tgkill thread-level or process-group signal syscall implementation.
answer.notes: The code has clone support, but no separate thread-directed signal syscall was found.

evidence.excerpt: INTERRUPT_FLAG; INTR_SOFTWARE/TIMER/EXTERNAL; EXCP_INST/LOAD/STORE/ENV_CALL/PAGE codes.
evidence.summary: Trap causes are classified by scause numeric encodings.

evidence.excerpt: cause = r_scause(); if cause == EXCP_ENV_CALL ... else if handle_intr(cause) ... else if handle_excp(cause)
evidence.summary: usertrap reads scause and software-dispatches by cause.

evidence.excerpt: handle_intr matches INTR_TIMER/external/software; handle_excp switches page/access faults.
evidence.summary: Interrupts and exceptions are separated by scause values.

answer.value: 通过 scause/mcause/VBAR 中断原因寄存器区分（硬件编码原因号）
answer.notes: In this RISC-V code the concrete register is scause; stvec is a shared entry selected for kernel/user context, then software dispatches by scause.

evidence.excerpt: kerneltrap saves sepc/sstatus/scause, asserts SPP and asserts intr_get()==0, handles trap, restores sepc and sstatus.
evidence.summary: Kernel trap handler expects interrupts disabled and restores prior sstatus.

evidence.excerpt: For syscalls only, after epc += 4, comment says don't enable until done with registers; intr_on(); syscall();
evidence.summary: Interrupts are re-enabled for syscall handling, not for general interrupt nesting.

evidence.excerpt: handle_intr handles timer/external/software interrupts without intr_on or nested_irq logic.
evidence.summary: Interrupt handler body does not re-enable interrupts.
Searched enable_irq_in_handler, nested_irq, nested interrupt, intr_on in handler paths; no explicit nested interrupt support was found.

answer.value: not_found
answer.notes: The kernel trap path asserts interrupts are disabled; handle_intr does not re-enable interrupts. intr_on appears in syscall handling after the syscall cause is separated, not as nested interrupt support inside interrupt handlers.

unknowns: No firmware source for sbi/sbi-k210 or sbi/sbi-qemu was present in the allowed source file listing, so internal SBI M-to-S implementation details are documented/build-evidenced rather than source-verified.
Syscall coverage has an interval because several compatibility syscalls validate arguments but intentionally implement only shallow semantics.
TrapFrame storage choice is slightly mismatched with the questionnaire choices: code kmallocs p->trapframe but trampoline uses it as per-process trampoline-accessible state.
